# Django-CRUM

Django-CRUM (Current Request User Middleware) captures the current request and
user in thread local storage.

It enables apps to check permissions, capture audit trails or otherwise access
the current request and user without requiring the request object to be passed
directly. It also offers a context manager to allow for temporarily
impersonating another user.

It provides a signal to extend the built-in function for getting the current
user, which could be helpful when using custom authentication methods or user
models.

Documentation can be found at [RTFD](http://django-crum.readthedocs.io/).

It is tested against:
: - Django 1.11 (Python 3.5 and 3.6)
  - Django 2.0 (Python 3.5, 3.6 and 3.7)
  - Django 2.1 (Python 3.5, 3.6 and 3.7)
  - Django 2.2 (Python 3.5, 3.6, 3.7, 3.8 and 3.9)
  - Django 3.0 (Python 3.6, 3.7, 3.8 and 3.9)
  - Django 3.1 (Python 3.6, 3.7, 3.8 and 3.9)
  - Django master/3.2 (Python 3.6, 3.7, 3.8 and 3.9)

functions.name: crum.impersonate
documentation: Temporarily impersonate the given user for audit trails.

functions.name: crum.get_current_request
documentation: Return the request associated with the current thread.

functions.name: crum.set_current_request
documentation: Update the request associated with the current thread.

functions.name: crum.get_current_user
documentation: Return the user associated with the current request thread.

functions.name: crum.set_current_user
documentation: Update the user associated with the current request thread.

classes.name: crum.CurrentRequestUserMiddleware
documentation: Middleware to capture the request and user from the current thread.

functions.name: crum.signals._get_current_user_from_request
documentation: Signal handler to retrieve current user from request.

functions.name: crum.signals._get_current_user_from_thread_locals
documentation: Signal handler to retrieve current user from thread locals.

functions.name: crum.signals._set_current_user_on_request
documentation: Signal handler to store current user to request.

functions.name: crum.signals._set_current_user_on_thread_locals
documentation: Signal handler to store current user on thread locals.
